prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow step that uses actions/github-script to do two things in one script: (1) using the workflow's GITHUB_TOKEN, add the label 'triage' to the issue/PR that triggered the run; (2) using a separate PAT stored as the secret CROSS_ORG_PAT, dispatch a 'trigger-deploy' repository_dispatch event to my-org/another-repo. Show the complete workflow step (just the step, not a full workflow) and explain briefly why you chose that approach for the second client.
assertions: Uses injected getOctokit factory (not github.getOctokit, not require)
Passes cross-org PAT via env: instead of inlining ${{ }} in script body
Notes mention v9 ESM-only / multi-token rationale for getOctokit
Step performs both addLabels and createDispatchEvent
